library_preparation_preflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is required, but meaningful jetty-webapp coverage needs tests to create a local webapp/WAR-like directory and start a WebAppContext rather than only instantiating classes.
library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Build tests around embedded Jetty usage: create a temporary webapp directory with WEB-INF/web.xml and simple resources, configure WebAppContext with setContextPath("/") and setWar(tempDir.toString()), attach it to a Server using port 0, start it, assert the context is available and/or serve a local request, then always stop/destroy the server. Prefer descriptor-based servlet/webapp coverage because org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-webapp already brings jetty-xml, jetty-servlet, jetty-security, jetty-server, jetty-http, jetty-io, jetty-util, and javax.servlet-api transitively.
risks: JSP support is optional and not present with the base jetty-webapp artifact; tests that exercise JSPs would need additional Jetty JSP modules and should be avoided unless explicitly required.
Annotation, JNDI/Plus, JMX, and WebSocket scenarios are optional Jetty integrations outside the base artifact; descriptor-based WebAppContext coverage is the safer default.
Use an ephemeral port and local temporary files only; avoid fixed ports or external services.
